baseline.model: opus-4.8/high planner, haiku-4.5/low execution (per-role routing on main)

pieces.name: peek-the-page replan
pieces.evidence: replaces the context-free no-op replan with a page-grounded suffix; wires the previously-inert replanner tier (opus-4.8/xhigh); improves pydocs 0->~40% but NOT reproducibly; no regression; M3 flat

pieces.name: planner-sees-start-url
pieces.evidence: tells the planner the start page so it stops navigating to a site's generic portal; flips wikipedia_search_submit + wikipedia_signin_synonym F->T (3/3 each); recovers the opus portal-navigation regression; no regression; M3 flat

pieces.name: relative-URL resolution
pieces.evidence: resolves site-relative navigate URLs against the current page; fixes the relative-URL regression B induced on pydocs; absolute/data: pass through unchanged

hard_gaps: pydocs_json_nav: flaky ~40% (A helps but not reliably)
gnu_licenses_nav: gnu.org UNREACHABLE from this environment (HTTP 000) - cannot validate here
separate_finding_for_user: The opus-4.8 planner upgrade (already merged to main, NOT round-3) is a MIXED tradeoff vs gpt-5.4: better synonym planning, but it navigates to site portals (broke wikipedia, recovered by B) and completes the wrong modal action -> a NEW silent failure on internet_modal (nominal=T verified=F, 2/2 on main). Decision point: keep opus+B+C, or reconsider the planner default / test sonnet-4.6. Mitigation for the modal silent failure = predict-then-verify (a future piece).
discipline: honesty gates kept: state_check frozen; no assert loosened; M3 not raised by A/B/C; kept only on independent verified-flips (wikipedia 3/3); pydocs not claimed as fixed; never pushed (worktree branch).
